Skip to content
PolyKybd
GitHub

What is PolyKybd?

A split ergonomic keyboard where every key has its own tiny OLED display.
Start Building Get a Kit

What makes it unique

Section titled “What makes it unique”

72 per-key displays

Every single key has a 0.42” OLED screen showing its current function — no more guessing what your layers do.

Fully split ergonomic

Two halves connected by a USB-C cable, letting you position each hand exactly where it feels natural.

Context-aware overlays

The host software tracks your active window and updates every key label automatically when you switch apps.

Open hardware

Full KiCad schematics, gerber files, STL case files, and QMK firmware — everything is open.

The ecosystem

Section titled “The ecosystem”

PolyKybd is made up of four open-source components that work together:

ComponentWhat it is
PolyKybd hardwareKiCad PCBs, 3D-printable case and key stems, assembly guide
QMK firmwareThe keyboard firmware running on the RP2040 microcontroller
PolyKybdHostPython host app (Windows / macOS / Linux) that drives the per-key displays
Adafruit GFX (fork)Rendering library used by the firmware to draw icons and text on the displays

Where to start

Section titled “Where to start”
  • Want to build one? → Assembly Overview
  • Already have one? → Install PolyKybdHost
  • Want to customize? → Keymaps & Layers
  • Want to develop? → Development

Support the project

Section titled “Support the project”

PolyKybd is an independent open hardware project. If you find it useful, consider supporting it:
